metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=ready — UNBLOCKED by subagent linking (antigravity_subagent.go). agy spawns a subagent via define_subagent + invoke_subagent; the child runs as its OWN top-level conversation brain/<child>/, discovered as its own session (transcript_new). AntigravityParentConvID scans sibling brain transcripts for the parent whose INVOKE_SUBAGENT step references the child's conversationId and emits parent_linked binding child→parent (wired in helpers.go deriveParentSession L102). The parent's subagent summary counts the child and the parent is held working while the child runs. Use a capable model (Gemini 3.1 Pro High / 3.5 Flash) — GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** `agy` v1.0.10 carries the `run_subagent` tool ('invokes one or more subagents by name with a single tool call; each runs with its own prompt and reports back when done') plus `define_subagent`/`invoke_subagent` and the `GetSubagents`/`HasSubagents`/`KillSubagent`/`SubagentSpec` symbols. A parent spawns a subagent via `define_subagent` + `invoke_subagent` (args.Subagents=[{TypeName,Role,Prompt}]) → an `INVOKE_SUBAGENT` result carrying the child `conversationId` → a new `brain/<child>/` dir. In the foreground shape the parent waits for the child to finish, then continues. Requires a CAPABLE model (Gemini 3.1 Pro High or 3.5 Flash); GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema.

**daemon=full (UNBLOCKED).** Previously frozen `incapable` on the (correct-at-the-time) finding that a subagent left no separate transcript and the adapter declared no parent link. Both halves are now resolved: (1) a subagent runs as its OWN top-level conversation `brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl`, which `FilesUnderRoot` + `sessionIDFromPath` (adapter.go) mints as its own session — the fswatcher emits `transcript_new` for the child. (2) `AntigravityParentConvID` (antigravity_subagent.go) derives the link: the parent runs as its own conversation and writes an `INVOKE_SUBAGENT` step whose content carries `"conversationId": "<child>"`; the resolver scans the most-recently-active sibling brain transcripts (windowed to the child's birth, tail-read) for the one referencing the child's conv-id and returns it as the parent. This is wired into the session detector's parent-derivation chain via `deriveParentSession` (helpers.go:102), so the detector emits `parent_linked` binding `child.session_id → parent.session_id`. The standard subagent machinery then applies: `ComputeSubagentSummary` counts the live child (SubagentCount(parent)=1), `reevaluateParent` holds the parent `working` while it has an active child, and the parent settles `working → ready` only after the child ends. LIVE-VERIFIED: a spawned subagent linked child.parent_session_id=parent, parent summary total:1 working:1, parent held working.

**driver=ready.** The recipe is a single `send` (the spawn prompt) + `wait_turn` + a settle `sleep`. All implemented in `driver-interactive.sh` (`send`→send_text, `wait_turn`→turn-count poll, `sleep`). No `keys`/`interrupt`/`sigkill` needed here.
caveats: daemon=full is derived from reading the linking code + the LIVE-VERIFIED note, not yet a committed recording; the parent_linked emission + held-working window are PROVISIONAL until record promotes them. confidence 0.75 reflects this.

The child runs inside the parent's invoke and has no own agy process, so the child session stays pid=0 (transcript-first; first-class for this adapter). Assert ParentSessionID linkage + subagent summary, NOT a child PID.

AntigravityParentConvID relies on the parent's INVOKE_SUBAGENT line being on disk before the child session is created (the detector's processing latency guarantees this per the antigravity_subagent.go doc); the recording must confirm parent_linked actually fires.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage.

sources.note: AntigravityParentConvID scans sibling brain transcripts for the parent whose INVOKE_SUBAGENT step references the child's conversationId — the link that flips this cell
deriveParentSession calls AntigravityParentConvID — the wiring point into the parent-derivation chain
FilesUnderRoot + sessionIDFromPath mints the child's own brain/<child>/transcript.jsonl as a session → transcript_new for the child
run_subagent / define_subagent / invoke_subagent / GetSubagents / SubagentSpec — the foreground-subagent feature

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
A CAPABLE model selected (Gemini 3.1 Pro High or Gemini 3.5 Flash via /model) — GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
A small file in the workspace cwd for the child to read
setup: Driver launches agy under tmux in a fresh workspace cwd; the parent conv-id session binds pid>0 (history.jsonl cwd).
The single send instructs the parent to spawn ONE subagent that reads a file and reports back, then the parent replies 'done' once the child finishes (foreground: parent waits).

script.text: Use a subagent to read the file ./note.txt and report its first line back to me. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply with exactly: done

verify: the parent conv-id session: ready → working at spawn
a child session appears (transcript_new for brain/<child>/transcript.jsonl)
SubagentCount(parent) = 1 while the child is alive; parent held working until the child ends
parent: working → ready only after the last child ends; final state ready
